title: Nested Conditionals
description: Place conditionals inside conditionals for complex logic.

slides.title: If Inside If
slides.content:
# Nesting

You can put an `if` statement inside another `if` (or inside `else`/`elseif`). This is useful when a condition depends on another condition.

But be careful: deep nesting can make code hard to read.

slides.title: Nested if Example
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$age = 20;
$hasID = true;

if ($age >= 18) {
    if ($hasID) {
        echo "You can enter.";
    } else {
        echo "You need ID.";
    }
} else {
    echo "You are too young.";
}
?>

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀

quizData.question: Nested conditionals are always the best way to handle multiple related conditions.
quizData.explanation: Often you can use logical operators or elseif to avoid deep nesting, which improves readability.
